UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the FynWealth application. This entity stores core user information linked to their authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

phoneNumber.description: The user's phone number, used for authentication (handled externally by Firebase).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

Bill.title: Bill
Bill.description: Represents a recurring bill or subscription that a user needs to track and manage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bill entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this bill belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Bill)

name.description: A descriptive name for the bill (e.g., 'Electricity Bill', 'Netflix Subscription').

amount.description: The monetary amount of the bill.

currency.description: The currency of the bill amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR'). Defaults to a primary currency if not specified by user.

dueDate.description: The date when the bill is due.

isPaid.description: Indicates whether the current instance of the bill has been paid.

notes.description: Any additional notes or details about the bill.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the bill record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

lastPaidDate.description: The date when the bill was last paid.

nextDueDate.description: The calculated next due date for the recurring bill.

ExpenseCategory.title: ExpenseCategory
ExpenseCategory.description: Defines categories for expenses, allowing users to organize and analyze their spending.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExpenseCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the expense category (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Transport', 'Dining Out').

description.description: A brief description of what expenses fall under this category.

isDefault.description: Indicates if this is a system-provided default category.

userId.description: Optional reference to the UserProfile who created this custom category. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ExpenseCategory)

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Represents an individual expenditure recorded by the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this expense belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Expense)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the expense.

currency.description: The currency of the expense amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR'). Defaults to a primary currency if not specified by user.

description.description: A brief description of the expense.

date.description: The date when the expense occurred.

expenseCategoryId.description: Reference to the ExpenseCategory this expense belongs to. (Relationship: ExpenseCategory 1:N Expense)

captureMethod.description: The method used to capture the expense (e.g., 'manual', 'scan', 'voice').

notes.description: Any additional notes about the expense.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the expense record was updated.

billImageUrl.description: URL to the image of the scanned bill or invoice, if applicable.

voiceNoteUrl.description: URL to the audio recording of the voice-captured expense, if applicable.

Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Represents a spending budget set by the user for a specific category or period.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Budget entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this budget belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Budget)

name.description: A descriptive name for the budget (e.g., 'Monthly Food Budget').

amount.description: The total monetary limit set for this budget.

currency.description: The currency of the budget amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR'). Defaults to a primary currency if not specified by user.

expenseCategoryId.description: Reference to the ExpenseCategory this budget applies to. (Relationship: ExpenseCategory 1:N Budget)

startDate.description: The start date of the budget period.

endDate.description: The end date of the budget period.

alertThreshold.description: A percentage (e.g., 80 for 80%) at which an alert should be triggered if the budget is nearing its limit.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the budget record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the budget record was updated.

Insight.title: Insight
Insight.description: Stores AI-generated financial insights and suggestions for the user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Insight entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this insight is for. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Insight)

type.description: The type of insight provided (e.g., 'Upcoming Heavy Month', 'Repeating Unnecessary Expense', 'Bill Prioritization').

title.description: A brief title summarizing the insight.

description.description: Detailed explanation of the insight and any smart suggestions.

generatedDate.description: Timestamp indicating when the insight was generated.

isDismissed.description: Indicates if the user has viewed and dismissed this insight.

relatedBillIds.description: An array of Bill IDs related to this insight, if applicable. (Relationship: Insight N:N Bill)

relatedExpenseIds.description: An array of Expense IDs related to this insight, if applicable. (Relationship: Insight N:N Expense)

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles, where the document ID matches the Firebase Authentication UID. Access is restricted to the user matching `userId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, which is also the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores recurring bills for a specific user. Each document includes the `userId` field, which must match the path's `userId` for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this bill.

params.name: billId
params.description: The unique identifier for the bill.

definition.description: Stores system-provided, read-only default expense categories accessible by all authenticated users.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the default expense category.

definition.description: Stores custom expense categories defined by a specific user. Each document includes the `userId` field, which must match the path's `userId` for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created this custom category.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the custom expense category.

definition.description: Stores individual expenditures recorded by a specific user. Each document includes the `userId` field, which must match the path's `userId` for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who recorded this expense.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier for the expense.

definition.description: Stores spending budgets set by a specific user. Each document includes the `userId` field, which must match the path's `userId` for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who created this budget.

params.name: budgetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the budget.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated financial insights and suggestions for a specific user. Each document includes the `userId` field, which must match the path's `userId` for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user for whom this insight was generated.

params.name: insightId
params.description: The unique identifier for the insight.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for FynWealth is designed to prioritize security, scalability, and debuggability in alignment with the core design principles. All user-specific data is organized under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection, followed by subcollections for individual user entities such as `bills`, `expenses`, `budgets`, and `insights`. This approach leverages path-based ownership, a highly secure and efficient pattern for managing private user data.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** Authorization independence is primarily achieved by structuring all user-owned data directly under the user's unique ID (`/users/{userId}/...`). For entities like `Bill`, `Expense`, `Budget`, and `Insight`, the document's `userId` field will always store the `uid` of the owning user. This `userId` field in the document itself, coupled with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, ensures that security rules do not need to perform expensive or complex `get()` operations on parent documents to determine ownership. A rule can simply check `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path wildcard) and `resource.data.userId == userId` (from the document's content). This makes authorization checks atomic and highly performant, directly satisfying the Authorization Independence mandate.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structural segregation inherently supports Queries as Authorization Policies (QAPs). For example, to retrieve all bills for the authenticated user, the client would query `firestore.collection('users').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('bills')`. The security rules for this path would then simply verify that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` in the path. This ensures that the client's query is implicitly authorized by its path, preventing over-fetching or unauthorized access attempts. Similarly, default expense categories are stored in a separate top-level collection (`/default_expense_categories`), allowing authenticated users to read them via a simple query without needing complex filtering rules.

**Structural Segregation:** Data with different security postures is strictly separated. For instance, system-wide `default_expense_categories` are in a top-level collection, readable by all authenticated users. In contrast, user-defined `expense_categories` are nested under `/users/{userId}/expense_categories`, ensuring only the owner can modify them. This clear separation simplifies security rules significantly by removing conditional logic based on data content within a single collection.

**Access Modeling:** Path-Based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) is consistently applied for all private user data (`UserProfile`, `Bill`, `Expense`, `Budget`, `Insight`, and user-defined `ExpenseCategory`). This makes ownership explicit and rules straightforward to write and debug. There is no collaborative data or custom claims based roles required by the current features, simplifying the model further.
